title: Xerisic Russian Egg Omelette
description: A light and floral Russian-inspired dish featuring miso-marinated eggs, perfect for a relaxing brunch. This low-calorie omelette is a great option for a healthy meal.
instructions: In a small bowl, whisk together the miso paste, olive oil, a pinch of salt, and a few grinds of black pepper until smooth.
Add the chopped dill and chives to the bowl and mix well.
Heat a small non-stick pan over medium heat.
Add the egg mixture to the pan and cook until the edges start to set, about 1-2 minutes.
Tilt the pan to gently lift and fold the edges of the omelette towards the center.
Use a spatula to carefully fold the omelette in half.
Cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, until the eggs are cooked through and the omelette is golden brown.
Transfer the omelette to a plate and garnish with grated lemon zest and a squeeze of lemon juice.
Serve warm, with a side of Greek yogurt if desired.
To make the miso marinade, whisk together the miso paste, lemon juice, and a pinch of salt in a small bowl until smooth.
Refrigerate the marinade for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Once the marinade has chilled, whisk it into the egg mixture before cooking the omelette.
